tasks.title: Initialize Project and Set Up Development Environment
tasks.description: Set up the project repository, install necessary dependencies (Python 3.8+, Node.js), and configure the development environment with SQLite for development and PostgreSQL for production.
tasks.details:
1. Create a new repository.
2. Initialize Python environment with `venv` and install FastAPI.
3. Initialize Node.js environment with `npm` and install React.
4. Configure SQLite for development and set up PostgreSQL connection strings for production.
5. Set up pre-commit hooks for code formatting and linting.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify that the development environment is correctly set up by running sample FastAPI and React applications. Confirm database connections to both SQLite and PostgreSQL.

tasks.title: Implement Rule Definition System
tasks.description: Develop a system for defining rules in JSON format that will drive the PDCA engine. This includes defining the JSON schema and creating an interface for managing rules.
tasks.details:
1. Define a JSON schema for rules, including conditions, actions, and priorities.
2. Implement an API endpoint in FastAPI to create, read, update, and delete rules.
3. Store rules in the SQLite database initially.
4. Implement validation logic to ensure rules conform to the defined schema.
tasks.testStrategy: Create unit tests to validate the JSON schema. Test API endpoints for creating, reading, updating, and deleting rules. Verify that invalid rules are rejected.

tasks.title: Develop Core PDCA Engine
tasks.description: Implement the core PDCA engine that executes based on the defined rules. This involves fetching relevant data, applying rules, and triggering actions.
tasks.details:
1. Implement a function to fetch relevant data based on the current context (e.g., project, repository).
2. Implement a rule engine that iterates through the defined rules and applies them based on the data.
3. Trigger actions based on the matched rules (e.g., create GitHub issues, update知見リポジトリ).
4. Log the execution history of the PDCA cycle.
tasks.testStrategy: Create integration tests to verify that the PDCA engine correctly applies rules and triggers actions. Test with different rule sets and data contexts.

tasks.title: Implement Basic Issue Management Functionality
tasks.description: Implement basic issue management functionality using the GitHub API. This includes creating, reading, updating, and closing issues.
tasks.details:
1. Implement functions to interact with the GitHub API for issue management.
2. Implement API endpoints to create, read, update, and close issues.
3. Store issue data in the database, mirroring relevant fields from the GitHub API.
4. Implement authentication using OAuth 2.0 or JWT.
tasks.testStrategy: Test the issue management API endpoints by creating, reading, updating, and closing issues in a test GitHub repository. Verify that the data is correctly stored in the database.

tasks.title: Implement Cursor MCP Integration
tasks.description: Integrate the system with the Cursor MCP to provide a seamless user experience. This involves creating a plugin or extension for Cursor that interacts with the system's API.
tasks.details:
1. Develop a Cursor plugin or extension that communicates with the system's API.
2. Implement UI elements within Cursor to trigger PDCA cycles, view issue status, and manage rules.
3. Handle authentication and authorization within the Cursor plugin.
4. Ensure seamless data synchronization between Cursor and the system.
tasks.testStrategy: Test the Cursor MCP integration by triggering PDCA cycles, viewing issue status, and managing rules from within Cursor. Verify that data is correctly synchronized.

tasks.title: Develop 知見 Management System
tasks.description: Develop a system for managing 知見 (knowledge) in a central repository. This includes defining a data model for 知見, implementing API endpoints for managing 知見, and creating a UI for browsing and searching 知見.
tasks.details:
1. Define a data model for 知見, including fields such as title, description, tags, and associated rules.
2. Implement API endpoints to create, read, update, and delete 知見.
3. Implement a search API to find relevant 知見 based on keywords and tags.
4. Store 知見 data in the database.
tasks.testStrategy: Test the 知見 management system by creating, reading, updating, and deleting 知見. Verify that the search API returns relevant results.

tasks.title: Implement Multi-Level PDCA
tasks.description: Implement multi-level PDCA execution across products, projects, and repositories. This involves extending the core PDCA engine to handle different contexts and scopes.
tasks.details:
1. Modify the PDCA engine to accept a context parameter that specifies the scope of execution (e.g., product, project, repository).
2. Implement logic to fetch relevant data based on the specified context.
3. Implement hierarchical rule execution, where rules can be defined at different levels and applied accordingly.
4. Update the UI to allow users to select the scope of PDCA execution.
tasks.testStrategy: Test the multi-level PDCA implementation by executing PDCA cycles at different levels (product, project, repository). Verify that the correct rules are applied and actions are triggered.

tasks.title: Develop Dashboard UI
tasks.description: Develop a dashboard UI to visualize the progress of PDCA cycles, issue status, and other relevant metrics. This involves creating React components and integrating with the system's API.
tasks.details:
1. Create React components to display PDCA cycle progress, issue status, and other metrics.
2. Implement API calls to fetch data from the system's API.
3. Design a user-friendly dashboard layout that provides a clear overview of the system's status.
4. Implement real-time updates using WebSockets or Server-Sent Events.
tasks.testStrategy: Test the dashboard UI by verifying that the correct data is displayed and that the UI is responsive and user-friendly. Test real-time updates.

tasks.title: Enhance Automation Features
tasks.description: Enhance the automation features of the system by adding support for more complex rules and actions. This includes implementing support for scripting languages and integrating with other tools and services.
tasks.details:
1. Implement support for scripting languages (e.g., Python, JavaScript) within rules.
2. Implement actions to integrate with other tools and services (e.g., Slack, Jira).
3. Implement a rule debugger to help users troubleshoot complex rules.
4. Add support for scheduling PDCA cycles.
tasks.testStrategy: Test the enhanced automation features by creating complex rules that use scripting languages and integrate with other tools and services. Verify that the rules are executed correctly and that the integrations work as expected.

tasks.title: Implement AI Cross-Cutting 知見 Sharing
tasks.description: Implement AI-powered cross-cutting 知見 sharing by leveraging AI APIs (e.g., Google Gemini, OpenAI) to analyze 知見 and suggest relevant connections and insights.
tasks.details:
1. Integrate with AI APIs to analyze 知見 and extract relevant information.
2. Implement a recommendation engine to suggest related 知見 based on the current context.
3. Implement a UI to display AI-powered insights and recommendations.
4. Train AI models to improve the accuracy of the recommendations.
tasks.testStrategy: Test the AI-powered 知見 sharing by verifying that the AI APIs are correctly integrated and that the recommendations are relevant and accurate. Test the UI for displaying insights and recommendations.

tasks.title: Develop Advanced Analysis and Reporting Features
tasks.description: Develop advanced analysis and reporting features to provide insights into the performance of PDCA cycles and the overall development process. This includes implementing support for custom reports and data visualizations.
tasks.details:
1. Implement support for custom reports that allow users to define the data and format of the reports.
2. Implement data visualizations to display key metrics and trends.
3. Implement a reporting API to allow users to programmatically generate reports.
4. Integrate with data analysis tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI).
tasks.testStrategy: Test the advanced analysis and reporting features by creating custom reports and data visualizations. Verify that the reports are accurate and that the visualizations are clear and informative.

tasks.title: Support Other Editors (Windsurf, etc.)
tasks.description: Extend the system to support other editors besides Cursor, such as Windsurf. This involves creating plugins or extensions for these editors that interact with the system's API.
tasks.details:
1. Develop plugins or extensions for other editors that communicate with the system's API.
2. Implement UI elements within the editors to trigger PDCA cycles, view issue status, and manage rules.
3. Handle authentication and authorization within the editor plugins.
4. Ensure seamless data synchronization between the editors and the system.
tasks.testStrategy: Test the editor integrations by triggering PDCA cycles, viewing issue status, and managing rules from within the editors. Verify that data is correctly synchronized.

tasks.title: Implement Enterprise Features
tasks.description: Implement enterprise features such as user management, access control, and auditing. This involves adding support for different user roles and permissions, and implementing logging and monitoring.
tasks.details:
1. Implement user management features to create, read, update, and delete users.
2. Implement access control features to restrict access to sensitive data and functionality.
3. Implement auditing features to track user activity and system events.
4. Integrate with enterprise authentication systems (e.g., LDAP, Active Directory).
tasks.testStrategy: Test the enterprise features by verifying that user management, access control, and auditing are working correctly. Test the integration with enterprise authentication systems.

tasks.title: Optimize Performance and Scalability
tasks.description: Optimize the performance and scalability of the system to handle large amounts of data and traffic. This involves profiling the code, identifying bottlenecks, and implementing caching and other optimization techniques.
tasks.details:
1. Profile the code to identify performance bottlenecks.
2. Implement caching to reduce database load.
3. Optimize database queries.
4. Implement load balancing to distribute traffic across multiple servers.
5. Monitor system performance and identify areas for improvement.
tasks.testStrategy: Test the performance and scalability of the system by running load tests and stress tests. Verify that the system can handle large amounts of data and traffic without performance degradation.

tasks.title: Implement Security Measures
tasks.description: Implement security measures to protect the system from unauthorized access and data breaches. This includes implementing authentication, authorization, and encryption.
tasks.details:
1. Implement authentication using OAuth 2.0 or JWT.
2. Implement authorization to restrict access to sensitive data and functionality.
3. Encrypt sensitive data at rest and in transit.
4. Implement security audits to identify and address vulnerabilities.
5. Implement intrusion detection and prevention systems.
tasks.testStrategy: Test the security measures by performing penetration tests and vulnerability scans. Verify that the system is protected from unauthorized access and data breaches.

tasks.title: Integrate Issue Management with PDCA Cycle
tasks.description: Integrate the issue management system with the PDCA cycle to automatically create and track issues based on PDCA execution results. This involves linking PDCA cycle actions to issue creation and updates.
tasks.details:
1. Modify the PDCA engine to include an option to create GitHub issues based on the outcome of the 'Act' phase.
2. Implement logic to automatically create issues in the GitHub repository when a PDCA cycle identifies a problem or area for improvement.
3. Store the association between PDCA cycle executions and created issues in the database.
4. Implement functionality to automatically update issue status (e.g., close issue) when the corresponding PDCA cycle reaches a successful conclusion.
5. Ensure that issue creation and updates are configurable via the rule definition system, allowing users to specify when and how issues should be created.
6. Implement error handling and logging to track issue creation and updates.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Create integration tests to verify that issues are automatically created in the GitHub repository when a PDCA cycle identifies a problem.
2. Verify that the issue description and other relevant fields are correctly populated based on the PDCA cycle context.
3. Test the functionality to automatically update issue status when the corresponding PDCA cycle reaches a successful conclusion.
4. Test the configuration options in the rule definition system to ensure that issue creation and updates can be customized.
5. Verify that error handling and logging are working correctly.
